Feedback: Agradecemos o envio, Socially Acceptable. Sua produção estabelece uma sonoridade dançante e autêntica, com um instrumental rico em baixos e trompetes que servem de base ideal para segundas vozes muito bem posicionadas aos 1:46. Contudo, a composição carrega uma energia bastante elevada que destoa da nossa curadoria atual, apresentando um vocal potente e percussões agitadas que criam um vigor acima do equilíbrio que buscamos hoje. Por essa força e presença marcante, seu som tem um encaixe excelente em seleções de indie pop que foquem em atmosferas vibrantes e enérgicas. Sucesso com seu lançamento e em toda a sua trajetória artística!

Song: Socially Acceptable - Me To Tell You
Socially Acceptable - Me To Tell You
Feedback:
Olá Socially Acceptable, esperamos que esteja tudo bem por aí.

Obrigado por compartilhar “Me To Tell You” com a nossa curadoria. A letra traz uma abordagem leve e descontraída, explorando identidade e individualidade de forma simples e direta. Há um charme na repetição e nas imagens cotidianas, que ajudam a construir uma narrativa acessível e com um toque divertido.

A faixa possui uma energia bem presente, com um andamento dinâmico que reforça essa vibe mais natural e envolvente ao longo da música.

Por outro lado, ao considerarmos dentro das nossas direções estéticas atuais, sentimos que o embasamento mais voltado aos anos 60 — especialmente com o uso de horns — em conjunto com uma mixagem vocal mais contida, acaba direcionando a faixa para um território mais retrô. Embora seja uma proposta bem executada e coerente, esse caráter acaba se distanciando do perfil que estamos priorizando no momento.

Desejamos muito sucesso com o lançamento e seguimos na torcida pelos próximos projetos!

All the best,
John V. – Music Producer & Curator at Indie Space Records

SH2BA comes out the gates hot and never really looks back. Huge driven guitar waves. Lots of forward momentum to the track. Rolling textured keys. Really strong lead vocals that sit on top the mix with passion and power. Solid bass low end. Very proggy arrangement. Lyrics feel like a stream of personal consciousness. Huge driven guitar waves. Its almost dynamic at its own expense, never allowing the listener to fully sink into the listen. Of course that's a matter of subjective tastes. I appreciate the listen here but i couldn't totally get into it.

Hey thanks for submitting this track. We enjoy the drive and energy that you have captured here. Especially the drums and the voice carry the track well together. That being said personally we are looking for a somewhat more intimate and moodier sound. So, sadly we have to pass this time. Much success with this song anyway!

Thanks for sharing your song SH2BA.  It had a smooth, classic indie feel to it.  Unfortunately I felt the style was a little too indie style for our hard rock and modern rock playlists - we look for more mainstream rock energy in the songs we put on the playlists - going to pass on this one, but feel free to send me others if you think they may fit.

Thanks for another submission!  Really enjoyed this song again, I thought your writing was really strong and all of the performances and production sounded great. Hi, I like the instrumental with upbeat melodies but unfortunately for my playlists I am looking for more moody songs rather than energetic ones. Sorry and good luck with promoting

Hi there

Thank you for your submission. Excellent smooth bassline groove here alongside the bright jangly guitar tones. I was a particular fan of the crisp snap on the percussion as well. However for our indie rock playlist we are looking for songs that have a more raucous energetic high tempo sound closer to the early releases of Arctic Monkeys and their song 'From The Ritz To The Rubble'. A very enjoyable listen though.

Best of luck with your continued promotional efforts for the release.

Kind Regards
Andrew

Valorizamos muito seus vocais, mas, nesta faixa, eles acabaram soando um pouco contidos em relação à potência da banda. É apenas uma questão de alinhamento com a energia que buscamos hoje. Embora não tenhamos conseguido encaixá-la desta vez, esperamos poder trabalhar juntos em um próximo lançamento.

Pleasant song you have here! Guitar riffs are cool along the way and vocals are great. I am looking for more aggressive indie rock/post punk tracks for this playlist though, which mood is not quite a fit so I will have to pass. Thank you for sharing though!

Hey, thank you so much for the submission! We really appreciate it. This was a fun song, and the production was excellent. Nice work putting it together! We enjoyed the vocal performance as well, but unfortunately, this is more on the adult contemporary and traditional pop rock side for our indie channel. So sorry that we'll have to decline due to genre, but we wish you the best and really hope that this finds a home on many playlists. Take care, and feel free to send anything in the future. 😊

Hi, thanks for sending this over! Loving the guitar work here! Has a bit of a Stevie Wonder feel on the verse! It's a cool track, it's just a bit too upbeat for me. The vocals on this track are powerful and the chorus is catchy, but the rest of the song is too classic-rock leaning, especially guitar-wise. Thanks for sending this across though!
killer guitar work here and the vocals are on point. unfortunately this one is more of a bluesy rock vibe rather than the indie folk im looking for but this was really great! thanks for sending!

Thank you for sharing this song with us! To be honest, this one feels a bit too familiar for me to accept. I love the gentle melodies and how the hard-hitting drums juxtapose them. I was hoping to hear that same nuanced individuality applied to the melodic phrasing of the song, so I'm going to pass respectfully. Consider me a fan, though. Please send more music our way soon!

Shoes That Float didn't totally grab my ears with its melody lines but it's got some fun points of production none the less. Liked the rolling keys, great recordings there. Nicely balanced live kit drums with loads of push and pull to the energy. Lead vocals have loads of soul. Lyrics feel like a stream of personal consciousness. Textural guitar work. Clean mix and master to boot. It's missing a knockout hook for me and my tastes but of course those tastes are totally subjective. I appreciate the imaginative lyrics and overall progressive arrangement. Keep it up.

There is no denying that the voice is not only smooth but quite cool and hypnotizing, but unfortunately for me, there were moments within the instrumental arrangement that I felt sounded a bit familiar.

While there is a lot of talent and charm here, I felt that the arrangement sounded a bit too old school at times.
